steps.title: Calculate f(2)
steps.content:
First, substitute \(x = 2\) into the function \(f(x) = x + 6\). This gives us
$$f(2) = 2 + 6.$$
So, $$f(2) = 8.$$

steps.title: Calculate g(2)
steps.content:
Next, substitute \(x = 2\) into the function \(g(x) = 6x\). This gives us
$$g(2) = 6(2).$$
So, $$g(2) = 12.$$

steps.title: Find the Value of \(7f(2)-g(2)\)
steps.content:
Now, use the values we just found to evaluate
$$7f(2) - g(2).$$
Substituting in the previously calculated results $$= 7(8) - 12.$$
This simplifies to $$56 - 12 = 44.$$ That matches choice D — \textbf{D} is correct.
